reasoning: The Lean statement captures each element of the informal claim exactly: a finite group G with card G = 312 (the order 312 hypothesis), the existential over a prime p (p.Prime) dividing the order (p ∣ card G), and the existence of a Sylow p-subgroup P of G that is normal (P.Normal). The quantifier structure ∃ p, ∃ P : Sylow p G matches "for some prime p... has a normal Sylow p-subgroup." The `p.Prime ∧ p ∣ card G` conditions correctly restrict p to the primes 2, 3, 13 dividing 312, which is the standard reading of "prime p dividing its order." No hypotheses are added or dropped, and no quantifier or type has drifted.
